name: everything_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_1_everything_aligned(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
    ensures
        aligned(addr, size),
{
}
reason: Would mean every address is aligned to every size, making the spec vacuously permissive

name: only_zero_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_2_only_zero_aligned(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
        aligned(addr, size),
    ensures
        addr == 0nat,
{
}
reason: Would mean only zero can ever be aligned, collapsing all non-trivial alignment to false

name: odd_aligned_to_even
reason: 1 is not a multiple of 2, so claiming 1 is 2-aligned indicates a broken modulus check

name: bad_product_composition
code:
proof fn phi_4_bad_product_composition(a: nat, b: nat, c: nat)
    requires
        b > 0,
        c > 0,
        aligned(a, b),
        aligned(a, c),
    ensures
        aligned(a, b * c),
{
}
reason: aligned(12,4) && aligned(12,6) does not imply aligned(12,24); conflates LCM with product

name: aligned_symmetric
code:
proof fn phi_5_aligned_symmetric(a: nat, b: nat)
    requires
        a > 0,
        b > 0,
        aligned(a, b),
    ensures
        aligned(b, a),
{
}
reason: aligned(4,2) is true but aligned(2,4) is false; symmetry would indicate modulus is broken
